title: Garage
description: Lightweight, self-hosted, S3-compatible distributed object storage built for geo-redundant clusters on commodity hardware.
installation: Garage is a single self-contained Rust daemon that keeps all metadata and object data on local disk, so it needs NO database add-on, just the two persistent volumes mounted below (/var/lib/garage/meta and /var/lib/garage/data). The S3 API is served on the container HTTP port 3900; the static web endpoint is 3902 and the admin API is 3903. Garage requires a few settings (rpc_secret, replication factor, bind addresses, dirs) that are normally supplied via /etc/garage.toml; the GARAGE_* env vars below cover the env-config path, but you should mount a garage.toml with replication_factor=1 for a single-node deploy. CRITICAL: rotate GARAGE_RPC_SECRET (a 32-byte hex value, generate with `openssl rand -hex 32`) and GARAGE_ADMIN_TOKEN / GARAGE_METRICS_TOKEN before exposing. First-run setup is CLI-driven: exec into the pod and run `garage layout assign -z dc1 -c 10G <node_id>` then `garage layout apply --version 1`, then create a key (`garage key create`) and a bucket (`garage bucket create`) and grant access. Point S3 clients at the app URL with path-style addressing. Garage is the object store itself, so it has no companion services.
